meta.purpose: Every designer-replaceable art slot this feature ships. All slots ship with procedural placeholders (the game's icon compositor or existing GLBs), so nothing blocks on art. Designers replace slots by delivering files per deliveryFormat; the pipeline and fallback behavior below mirror the shipped deeds icon pipeline (docs/achievements/icon-brief.md is the precedent).

deliveryFormat.icons: 512x512 transparent-background PNG, sRGB, painted style matching the existing item/deed icon sets, named exactly <asset id>.png. The converter downscales to a 128px square WebP (q82) served from public/ui/<set>/; WebP becomes the committed source of truth. Icons must read clearly at 46px (desktop button) and 40px (mobile).
deliveryFormat.worldProps: GLB (glTF binary), CC0 or licensed with a CREDITS.md row, low-poly matching the KayKit-adjacent world style. The models/props asset-budget group allows 4 MiB total; every GLB needs a media-manifest regen and a registerPreload entry.
deliveryFormat.chromeGlyphs: Monochrome inline SVG (single path preferred), added to the svgIcon registry; used only for secondary controls, never primary destinations.

meta.pipeline: Icons: drop PNGs into public/ui/professions/ (new set), run the professions converter (cloned from scripts/convert_item_icons_webp.mjs at Phase 5; regenerates the checked-in id set; a bijection test pins art to wiring). Item-family icons instead go to public/ui/items/ via npm run assets:items and ITEM_IMAGE_IDS. Deed crests go to public/ui/deeds/ via npm run assets:deeds.
meta.fallback: Every icon id has a procedural recipe (icons.ts r() helper) or an automatic kind/keyword fallback, so an unshipped slot renders a coherent placeholder, never a blank.
meta.styleNotes: Classic dark-fantasy: blue-black grounds, antique gold accents, warm parchment light. No emoji-style art, no flat modern glyphs for painted slots, no text baked into icons (localization). Palette and mood notes per asset below.

categories.name: Profession icons (painted, public/ui/professions/)
categories.note: Used as the wheel-window ring nodes, crafting-window section headers, and tooltip headers. One per craft on the ten-craft wheel; the six deep crafts are priority 1, the shallow three priority 3 (engineering counts as deep for tools).

assets.name: Weaponcrafting
assets.description: A sword blade mid-forge across an anvil horn, ember glow
assets.palette: steel blue, ember orange, antique gold rim

assets.name: Armorcrafting
assets.description: A breastplate with a raised hammer, rivet detail

assets.name: Tailoring
assets.description: A bone needle drawing gold thread through folded cloth

assets.name: Leatherworking
assets.description: A curved skinning knife over a stretched hide

assets.name: Cooking
assets.description: A steaming ladle over a hearth pot

assets.name: Alchemy
assets.description: A round-bottom flask mid-swirl with rising motes

assets.name: Engineering
assets.description: Interlocked brass gears with a small wrench

assets.name: Enchanting
assets.description: A rune-etched rod trailing arcane ribbon

assets.name: Jewelcrafting
assets.description: A faceted gem in a jeweler's clamp catching light

assets.name: Inscription
assets.description: A quill inking a glowing glyph on parchment

assets.name: Mining
assets.description: A pick striking an ore seam with a spark

assets.name: Logging
assets.description: A woodaxe sunk into a rings-visible stump

assets.name: Herbalism
assets.description: A sprig with three leaves and one luminous bloom

assets.name: Fishing
assets.description: A hooked fish curling above water rings

categories.name: Archetype crests (painted, public/ui/professions/)
categories.note: The identity art: shown on the wheel window identity panel, the attunement quest preview, and the title tooltip. Wave-one four are priority 1; the six future pairs are deferred but listed so the whole set can be commissioned in one sitting (deeds-brief convention).

assets.name: The Smith
assets.description: Crossed hammer and sword over an anvil, shield-crest framing
assets.palette: forge orange on gunmetal, gold frame

assets.name: The Outfitter
assets.description: Needle and skinning knife crossed over a folded cloak

assets.name: The Apothecary
assets.description: Flask pouring into a hearth pot, herb sprig behind

assets.name: The Bombardier
assets.description: A round bomb with a lit fuse over crossed gears

assets.name: Masterwork seal
assets.description: A small eight-point gold burst seal, legible as a corner overlay at 16 to 24px on top of any item icon; also shown large in the masterwork toast
assets.size: 512px source PNG; must survive downscale to 20px

assets.name: Maker's mark
assets.description: A tiny quill-stroke signature flourish rendered inline before the crafter's name in tooltips; monochrome SVG in the svgIcon registry (currentColor)

categories.name: Material item icons (painted, public/ui/items/ via npm run assets:items)
categories.note: Phase 4 and Phase 10 introduce real gathered materials and craft reagents. Exact item ids land with those phases and get appended here; every one has a procedural fallback from item kind + name keywords, so this batch is deliberately LAST to commission. Families and approximate counts below.

families.count: 6 to 9 (3 tiers x ore/bar, plus rare variants)

families.palette: greens with one luminous accent each

families.count: 4 to 6 (hide, silk, venom sac, fang, meat, replacing quest-item collisions)

categories.name: Station world props (GLB, public/models/props/)
categories.note: One prop per typed station, placed beside its master NPC. anvil.glb already exists (reuse for the forge); a campfire prop exists for field cooking. Keep the whole batch within the 4 MiB models/props budget; CC0 preferred with CREDITS.md rows.

assets.name: Forge station
assets.description: Anvil + quench barrel + small coal hearth cluster
assets.reuse: anvil.glb exists; hearth/barrel are the new pieces

assets.name: Kitchens station
assets.description: Brick stove with hanging pots and a prep table

assets.name: Apothecary station
assets.description: Bench with alembic, shelf of bottles

assets.name: Tannery station
assets.description: Hide stretching rack and a dye vat

assets.name: Loom station
assets.description: Standing loom with warp threads and a cloth roll

assets.name: Toolworks station
assets.description: Workbench with vise, gear pile, hanging tools

categories.size: GLB, each roughly 0.3 to 0.6 MiB, batch under the 4 MiB props budget

categories.name: Master NPC appearances
categories.note: Ships on existing KayKit character GLBs via NPC_KEYS (npc_smith and npc_villager exist). No new art required for wave one; optional bespoke costume textures listed as a deferred nice-to-have.

assets.name: Master NPC costume set
assets.description: Six themed tints/attachments (smith apron, cook's hat, alchemist satchel, tanner gloves, weaver shawl, engineer goggles) on the existing character rigs

categories.name: Deed crests for new profession deeds (public/ui/deeds/ via npm run assets:deeds)
categories.note: Follows the existing deeds icon brief format exactly (docs/achievements/icon-brief.md). Ids land with Phase 15; category crest fallback covers them until art arrives.

assets.name: First Craft
assets.description: A single finished sword on an anvil, first light

assets.name: First Masterwork
assets.description: The masterwork seal bursting over an open palm

assets.name: Attuned
assets.description: A guild letter with a wax seal, broken open

assets.name: The Rare Catch
assets.description: An iridescent koi mid-leap over moonlit water

assets.name: Tier milestone set (per-craft, count lands Phase 15)
Specialist (first 75-skill specialization, any craft)
assets.description: A masterfully kept tool resting on a velvet cloth

assets.name: Rare find set (pristine vein, ancient heartwood, moonlit bloom, perfect specimen)

categories.name: Minimap and map markers (painter work, not image files)
categories.note: Station markers render via the minimap painter with --color-minimap-* tokens (a new marker variant in the discriminated union), NOT as image assets. Listed here so designers know the shapes to expect and can propose glyph refinements later.

assets.name: Station marker
assets.description: A small anvil-silhouette dot variant per station type; must stay legible at minimap scale and identical across graphics tiers

assets.name: Pristine vein marker
assets.description: The existing gather-node marker with a brief glint accent (reduced-motion aware); the ore flavor of the rare-event marker family

assets.name: Ancient heartwood marker
assets.description: The wood flavor of the rare-event marker family, same glint treatment as the pristine vein

assets.name: Moonlit bloom marker
assets.description: The herb flavor of the rare-event marker family, same glint treatment as the pristine vein
